# Effectiveness of Office Hours
## Analysis
To get an understanding of the current usage and perceived effectiveness of the office hours, a survey was conducted. Of the students who conducted the survey, 291 students stated they attended office hours. The average effectiveness of the office hours was 5.1517 (on a scale of 1-7). Most people described office hours as being effective despite most students only attending once. The data shows that increasing the availability of office hours would increase the attendance.

|Figure 3. Shows the distribution of how students rated the effectiveness of office hours.|


## Conclusion
Most students describe having been to office hours 0-2 times during their time in COMP110. Of the students who attended office hours one time, the average rated efficacy is close to four (on a scale of 1-7). The students who attended 5+ times rate the efficacy of office hours at 5 and above. On the whole, most students find office hours to be effective. The provided hours should be increased to allow for more availability to students with busier schedules who may not be able to attend. Offering online office hours to students who live off campus and spend limited time on campus would likely improve attendance numbers.

More data should be gathered into why students didn't go to office hours or why they only attended 1-2 times. Understanding this would help provide better help to these students and improve the quality of service in office hours. Some students may not have been able to attend the available office hours due to the limited number of hours provided. Others may have been discouraged from attending again due to perceiving office hours as being unhelpful. Understanding the specifics of what is stopping students from attending would be imperative to increasing student attendance.

Increasing the number of office hours would require more volunteers. These volunteers would need to have different hours they are able to supply for office hours. There are also limitations in providing online office hours. The volunteers wouldn't have direct access to the students laptops. To account for this issue, online office hours should be limited to subjects that don't require the volunteers to have direct access to the students laptop.
